title: The Night Market Tofu Stall
text_zh: 夜市最里面有一个不太起眼的豆腐摊，只卖两样东西：臭豆腐和豆腐脑。摊主是一个五十多岁的阿姨，她每天下午两点就开始准备，磨豆子、做豆腐、调酱料，一直忙到傍晚五点夜市开始。她的臭豆腐炸得外面脆里面嫩，配上她自己做的辣酱，味道特别好。我问她为什么不多卖几样东西，她说：「做好两样就够了。什么都卖的话，什么都做不好。」她说这句话的时候很认真。排队的人总是很多，但是她从来不着急，每一份都做得很仔细。吃她的豆腐就像一种仪式，要慢慢地品尝。有些食物不需要很多选择，简单的、认真做的，就是最好的。
text_pinyin: Yèshì zuì lǐmiàn yǒu yí gè bú tài qǐyǎn de dòufu tān, zhǐ mài liǎng yàng dōngxi: chòu dòufu hé dòufu nǎo. Tānzhǔ shì yí gè wǔshí duō suì de āyí, tā měi tiān xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn jiù kāishǐ zhǔnbèi, mò dòuzi, zuò dòufu, tiáo jiàngliào, yìzhí máng dào bàngwǎn wǔ diǎn yèshì kāishǐ. Tā de chòu dòufu zhá de wàimiàn cuì lǐmiàn nèn, pèi shàng tā zìjǐ zuò de làjiàng, wèidào tèbié hǎo. Wǒ wèn tā wèi shénme bù duō mài jǐ yàng dōngxi, tā shuō: 「Zuò hǎo liǎng yàng jiù gòu le. Shénme dōu mài dehuà, shénme dōu zuò bu hǎo.」 Tā shuō zhè jù huà de shíhou hěn rènzhēn. Páiduì de rén zǒng shì hěn duō, dànshì tā cónglái bù zháojí, měi yí fèn dōu zuò de hěn zǐxì. Chī tā de dòufu jiù xiàng yì zhǒng yíshì, yào mànmàn de pǐncháng. Yǒuxiē shíwù bù xūyào hěn duō xuǎnzé, jiǎndān de, rènzhēn zuò de, jiù shì zuì hǎo de.
text_en: At the far end of the night market there's an unassuming tofu stall that sells only two things: stinky tofu and tofu pudding. The owner is a woman in her fifties who starts preparing at two every afternoon — grinding soybeans, making tofu, mixing sauces — busy until the market opens at five. Her stinky tofu is fried crispy on the outside and tender inside, and paired with her homemade chili sauce, the flavor is exceptional. I asked why she doesn't sell more items. She said: 'Doing two things well is enough. If you sell everything, you can't do anything well.' She was very serious when she said this. The line is always long, but she's never in a rush, preparing each portion carefully. Eating her tofu is like a ritual — you have to savor it slowly. Some foods don't need many choices; simple, made with care, is the best there is.

questions.q_zh: 阿姨的摊位卖几样东西？
questions.q_en: How many items does the woman's stall sell?

questions.q_zh: 阿姨为什么不多卖几样东西？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the woman sell more items?

options.text: 因为她没有钱买更多食材
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā méiyǒu qián mǎi gèng duō shícái

options.text: 做好两样就够了，什么都卖就做不好
options.pinyin: zuò hǎo liǎng yàng jiù gòu le, shénme dōu mài jiù zuò bu hǎo
options.text_en: doing two well is enough; selling everything means nothing is done well

options.pinyin: kèrén zhǐ xǐhuan zhè liǎng yàng

title: The Shared Dryer
text_zh: 梅雨季节的时候，公寓楼的公用烘干机变成了最受欢迎的东西。平时没什么人用，但是连续下了一个星期的雨以后，大家都需要烘干衣服。洗衣房里贴了一张纸，上面写着每家可以用四十分钟。有一天我去洗衣房的时候，发现一个女孩正坐在地上等烘干机。她告诉我已经等了快一个小时了，前面那个人用了很久。我们一起等的时候聊了起来。她刚搬来两个月，还不太认识这栋楼的人。我告诉她二楼的便利店周三打折，六楼的陈阿姨有时候会分享她种的菜。她听了觉得很开心，说：「原来这栋楼还挺有意思的。」后来我们偶尔在洗衣房碰到，都会聊上几句。一台烘干机让两个陌生人变成了可以说话的邻居。
text_pinyin: Méiyǔ jìjié de shíhou, gōngyù lóu de gōngyòng hōnggānjī biàn chéng le zuì shòu huānyíng de dōngxi. Píngshí méi shénme rén yòng, dànshì liánxù xià le yí gè xīngqī de yǔ yǐhòu, dàjiā dōu xūyào hōnggān yīfu. Xǐyīfáng lǐ tiē le yì zhāng zhǐ, shàngmiàn xiě zhe měi jiā kěyǐ yòng sìshí fēnzhōng. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ qù xǐyīfáng de shíhou, fāxiàn yí gè nǚhái zhèng zuò zài dìshang děng hōnggānjī. Tā gàosu wǒ yǐjīng děng le kuài yí gè xiǎoshí le, qiánmiàn nàge rén yòng le hěn jiǔ. Wǒmen yìqǐ děng de shíhou liáo le qǐlái. Tā gāng bān lái liǎng gè yuè, hái bú tài rènshi zhè dòng lóu de rén. Wǒ gàosu tā èr lóu de biànlìdiàn zhōusān dǎzhé, liù lóu de Chén āyí yǒu shíhou huì fēnxiǎng tā zhòng de cài. Tā tīng le juéde hěn kāixīn, shuō: 「Yuánlái zhè dòng lóu hái tǐng yǒu yìsi de.」 Hòulái wǒmen ǒu'ěr zài xǐyīfáng pèng dào, dōu huì liáo shàng jǐ jù. Yì tái hōnggānjī ràng liǎng gè mòshēng rén biàn chéng le kěyǐ shuō huà de línjū.
text_en: During the plum rain season, the apartment building's shared dryer becomes the most popular item. Normally few people use it, but after a week of continuous rain, everyone needs to dry their clothes. A notice in the laundry room says each household can use it for forty minutes. One day when I went to the laundry room, I found a girl sitting on the floor waiting for the dryer. She told me she'd been waiting almost an hour — the person before her had used it a long time. We started chatting while waiting together. She'd moved in just two months ago and didn't know many people in the building yet. I told her the convenience store on the second floor has discounts on Wednesdays, and Aunt Chen on the sixth floor sometimes shares vegetables she grows. She seemed happy to hear this and said: 'So this building is actually pretty interesting.' After that we'd occasionally run into each other in the laundry room and chat a bit. A single dryer turned two strangers into neighbors who could talk to each other.

questions.q_zh: 为什么梅雨季节烘干机特别受欢迎？
questions.q_en: Why is the dryer especially popular during plum rain season?

options.text: 因为连续下雨，大家都需要烘干衣服
options.pinyin: yīnwèi liánxù xià yǔ, dàjiā dōu xūyào hōnggān yīfu
options.text_en: continuous rain means everyone needs to dry clothes

questions.q_zh: 那个女孩搬来多久了？
questions.q_en: How long has the girl been living there?

title: The Park Bench in Autumn
text_zh: 秋天的时候，公园里的长椅变得更加安静了。夏天的时候到处都是人，但是天气一凉，坐在外面的人就少了。我喜欢秋天的长椅，因为你可以一个人坐很久也不会有人来打扰。有一天下午我坐在椅子上看书，一片黄叶飘下来落在了我的书页上。我把它夹在书里当作书签。旁边的椅子上坐着一个头发白了一半的女士，她在织一条围巾。织了一会儿她抬起头来，看着树上的叶子说：「今年的叶子比去年红得更早。」我说是的。我们没有继续说话，但是那一刻的安静让人觉得很舒服。秋天的公园不需要太多的话，树叶的颜色、凉爽的风、还有长椅上各自安静的人，这些就已经足够了。
text_pinyin: Qiūtiān de shíhou, gōngyuán lǐ de chángyǐ biàn de gèngjiā ānjìng le. Xiàtiān de shíhou dàochù dōu shì rén, dànshì tiānqì yí liáng, zuò zài wàimiàn de rén jiù shǎo le. Wǒ xǐhuan qiūtiān de chángyǐ, yīnwèi nǐ kěyǐ yí gè rén zuò hěn jiǔ yě bú huì yǒu rén lái dǎrǎo. Yǒu yì tiān xiàwǔ wǒ zuò zài yǐzi shàng kàn shū, yí piàn huáng yè piāo xiàlái luò zài le wǒ de shūyè shàng. Wǒ bǎ tā jiā zài shū lǐ dāng zuò shūqiān. Pángbiān de yǐzi shàng zuòzhe yí gè tóufa bái le yí bàn de nǚshì, tā zài zhī yì tiáo wéijīn. Zhī le yíhuìr tā tái qǐ tóu lái, kàn zhe shù shàng de yèzi shuō: 「Jīnnián de yèzi bǐ qùnián hóng de gèng zǎo.」 Wǒ shuō shì de. Wǒmen méiyǒu jìxù shuō huà, dànshì nà yí kè de ānjìng ràng rén juéde hěn shūfu. Qiūtiān de gōngyuán bù xūyào tài duō de huà, shùyè de yánsè, liángshuǎng de fēng, hái yǒu chángyǐ shàng gèzì ānjìng de rén, zhèxiē jiù yǐjīng zúgòu le.
text_en: In autumn, the park benches become even quieter. In summer there are people everywhere, but once the weather turns cool, fewer people sit outside. I like autumn benches because you can sit alone for a long time without anyone disturbing you. One afternoon I was sitting and reading when a yellow leaf drifted down and landed on my page. I tucked it inside the book as a bookmark. On the bench beside me sat a woman with half-gray hair, knitting a scarf. After knitting for a while she looked up at the leaves on the tree and said: 'This year's leaves turned red earlier than last year.' I said yes. We didn't continue talking, but that moment of quiet felt very comfortable. An autumn park doesn't need many words — the colors of the leaves, the cool breeze, and people sitting quietly on their benches — that's already enough.

questions.q_zh: 「我」用什么当书签？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' use as a bookmark?

questions.q_zh: 旁边的女士在做什么？
questions.q_en: What was the woman beside doing?

title: The Restaurant That Changes with Spring
text_zh: 春天一到，楼下的那家小饭馆就会在门口摆出一块小黑板，上面用粉笔写着这个月的新菜。三月是荠菜馄饨和香椿炒蛋，四月是春笋烧肉。老板说他最喜欢春天，因为春天的食材最丰富也最新鲜。他每个星期都会自己去郊外的农场买菜，回来以后根据买到了什么来决定做什么。所以你永远不知道下次来的时候菜单上会多出什么新菜。有一次他买到了很好的野菜，高兴得不得了，当天晚上做了一道从来没有出现在菜单上的凉拌野菜。他送了一小盘给我，味道清淡但是很香。他说：「好的食材不需要太多调料，简单地做就是对它最大的尊重。」
text_pinyin: Chūntiān yí dào, lóu xià de nà jiā xiǎo fànguǎn jiù huì zài ménkǒu bǎi chū yí kuài xiǎo hēibǎn, shàngmiàn yòng fěnbǐ xiě zhe zhège yuè de xīn cài. Sān yuè shì jìcài húntun hé xiāngchūn chǎo dàn, sì yuè shì chūnsǔn shāo ròu. Lǎobǎn shuō tā zuì xǐhuan chūntiān, yīnwèi chūntiān de shícái zuì fēngfù yě zuì xīnxiān. Tā měi gè xīngqī dōu huì zìjǐ qù jiāowài de nóngchǎng mǎi cài, huílái yǐhòu gēnjù mǎi dào le shénme lái juédìng zuò shénme. Suǒyǐ nǐ yǒngyuǎn bù zhīdào xià cì lái de shíhou càidān shàng huì duō chū shénme xīn cài. Yǒu yí cì tā mǎi dào le hěn hǎo de yěcài, gāoxìng de bùdéliǎo, dāng tiān wǎnshang zuò le yí dào cónglái méiyǒu chūxiàn zài càidān shàng de liángbàn yěcài. Tā sòng le yì xiǎo pán gěi wǒ, wèidào qīngdàn dànshì hěn xiāng. Tā shuō: 「Hǎo de shícái bù xūyào tài duō tiáoliào, jiǎndān de zuò jiù shì duì tā zuì dà de zūnzhòng.」
text_en: As soon as spring arrives, the small restaurant downstairs puts out a little blackboard by the entrance with this month's new dishes written in chalk. March brings shepherd's purse wontons and scrambled eggs with Chinese toon, April has braised pork with spring bamboo shoots. The owner says he loves spring best because the ingredients are richest and freshest. Every week he drives to a farm outside the city to buy produce himself, then decides what to cook based on what he found. So you never know what new dish will appear on the menu next time. Once he found some excellent wild greens and was absolutely delighted — that evening he made a cold wild greens dish that had never appeared on the menu before. He gave me a small plate; the taste was light but fragrant. He said: 'Good ingredients don't need much seasoning. Keeping it simple is the greatest respect you can show them.'

questions.q_zh: 老板怎么决定做什么菜？
questions.q_en: How does the owner decide what to cook?

options.text: 根据从农场买到了什么来决定
options.pinyin: gēnjù cóng nóngchǎng mǎi dào le shénme lái juédìng
options.text_en: based on what he buys from the farm

questions.q_zh: 老板觉得好食材应该怎么做？
questions.q_en: How does the owner think good ingredients should be prepared?

title: The Bicycle Repair Woman
text_zh: 地铁站外面有一个修自行车的阿姨。她每天早上八点准时到，晚上六点收摊。她的摊位很简单，一辆三轮车上放着工具箱和打气筒，旁边撑着一把大伞。我的自行车链条断了，别的地方修要五十块，她只收了十五块。修车的时候她跟我说她以前在工厂上班，工厂关了以后她就开始修自行车。她丈夫教她的——他以前就是干这行的。丈夫去世以后她接过了这个摊位。她说修车不难，难的是一年三百六十五天风吹日晒都在外面。但是她已经习惯了，每天能见到各种各样的人，听到各种各样的故事，日子也不无聊。她把修好的车还给我的时候说：「骑慢一点，链条新换的，要磨合几天。」
text_pinyin: Dìtiě zhàn wàimiàn yǒu yí gè xiū zìxíngchē de āyí. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang bā diǎn zhǔnshí dào, wǎnshang liù diǎn shōu tān. Tā de tānwèi hěn jiǎndān, yí liàng sānlún chē shàng fàng zhe gōngjùxiāng hé dǎqìtǒng, pángbiān chēng zhe yì bǎ dà sǎn. Wǒ de zìxíngchē liàntiáo duàn le, bié de dìfang xiū yào wǔshí kuài, tā zhǐ shōu le shíwǔ kuài. Xiū chē de shíhou tā gēn wǒ shuō tā yǐqián zài gōngchǎng shàng bān, gōngchǎng guān le yǐhòu tā jiù kāishǐ xiū zìxíngchē. Tā zhàngfu jiāo tā de — tā yǐqián jiù shì gàn zhè háng de. Zhàngfu qùshì yǐhòu tā jiē guò le zhège tānwèi. Tā shuō xiū chē bù nán, nán de shì yì nián sān bǎi liùshíwǔ tiān fēngchuī rìshài dōu zài wàimiàn. Dànshì tā yǐjīng xíguàn le, měi tiān néng jiàn dào gè zhǒng gè yàng de rén, tīng dào gè zhǒng gè yàng de gùshi, rìzi yě bù wúliáo. Tā bǎ xiū hǎo de chē huán gěi wǒ de shíhou shuō: 「Qí màn yìdiǎn, liàntiáo xīn huàn de, yào móhé jǐ tiān.」
text_en: Outside the subway station there's a woman who repairs bicycles. She arrives promptly at eight every morning and packs up at six in the evening. Her setup is simple — a tricycle carrying a toolbox and an air pump, with a large umbrella propped up beside it. My bicycle chain broke; other places wanted fifty yuan, but she only charged fifteen. While repairing it, she told me she used to work in a factory. After the factory closed, she started repairing bicycles. Her husband taught her — he used to be in the trade. After he passed away, she took over the stall. She says repairing bikes isn't hard; what's hard is being outside in wind and sun 365 days a year. But she's used to it — meeting all sorts of people and hearing all sorts of stories every day keeps life from being boring. When she returned my repaired bike she said: 'Ride slowly — the chain is newly replaced, it needs a few days to break in.'

questions.q_zh: 阿姨是怎么学会修自行车的？
questions.q_en: How did the woman learn to repair bicycles?

options.pinyin: zìjǐ zài wǎng shàng xué de

questions.q_zh: 阿姨觉得修车最难的是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the woman find hardest about bike repair?

options.text: 一年到头风吹日晒都在外面
options.pinyin: yì nián dào tóu fēngchuī rìshài dōu zài wàimiàn
options.text_en: being outdoors in all weather year-round

title: The Lost Umbrella
text_zh: 我有一把蓝色的折叠伞，用了三年了，伞柄上刻着我的名字。上个星期我在咖啡店忘了拿，回去找的时候已经不见了。我有点难过，虽然只是一把伞，但是用了很久就会有感情。过了几天，我去地铁站的失物招领处问了一下，没想到那把伞真的在那里。工作人员说有人在咖啡店捡到了，看到上面有名字，就送到了最近的失物招领处。伞上还挂着一张小纸条：「希望你的伞能找到回家的路。」我不知道是谁写的，但是看到这句话的时候鼻子有点酸。在一个大城市里，有人愿意为了一把别人的伞多走几步路，这件事本身就很了不起。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yì bǎ lán sè de zhédié sǎn, yòng le sān nián le, sǎn bǐng shàng kè zhe wǒ de míngzi. Shàng gè xīngqī wǒ zài kāfēi diàn wàng le ná, huíqù zhǎo de shíhou yǐjīng bújiàn le. Wǒ yǒudiǎn nánguò, suīrán zhǐ shì yì bǎ sǎn, dànshì yòng le hěn jiǔ jiù huì yǒu gǎnqíng. Guò le jǐ tiān, wǒ qù dìtiě zhàn de shīwù zhāolǐng chù wèn le yíxià, méi xiǎng dào nà bǎ sǎn zhēn de zài nàlǐ. Gōngzuò rényuán shuō yǒu rén zài kāfēi diàn jiǎn dào le, kàn dào shàngmiàn yǒu míngzi, jiù sòng dào le zuìjìn de shīwù zhāolǐng chù. Sǎn shàng hái guà zhe yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo: 「Xīwàng nǐ de sǎn néng zhǎo dào huí jiā de lù.」 Wǒ bù zhīdào shì shéi xiě de, dànshì kàn dào zhè jù huà de shíhou bízi yǒudiǎn suān. Zài yí gè dà chéngshì lǐ, yǒu rén yuànyì wèile yì bǎ biéren de sǎn duō zǒu jǐ bù lù, zhè jiàn shì běnshēn jiù hěn liǎobuqǐ.
text_en: I have a blue folding umbrella that I've used for three years, with my name engraved on the handle. Last week I forgot it at a coffee shop, and when I went back to look, it was gone. I was a little sad — even though it's just an umbrella, you develop feelings for something you've used so long. A few days later, I asked at the subway station's lost and found, and to my surprise, the umbrella was actually there. The staff said someone found it at the coffee shop, saw the name on it, and brought it to the nearest lost and found. There was even a small note hanging from it: 'I hope your umbrella finds its way home.' I don't know who wrote it, but when I read those words my nose tingled a bit. In a big city, someone willing to walk a few extra steps for another person's umbrella — that in itself is quite remarkable.

questions.q_zh: 伞柄上刻着什么？
questions.q_en: What is engraved on the umbrella handle?

questions.q_zh: 纸条上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the note say?

options.text: 希望你的伞能找到回家的路
options.pinyin: xīwàng nǐ de sǎn néng zhǎo dào huí jiā de lù
options.text_en: I hope your umbrella finds its way home

options.pinyin: qǐng dào kāfēi diàn lái qǔ
options.text_en: please come pick it up at the coffee shop

title: The Notice on the Bulletin Board
text_zh: 今天经过小区公告栏的时候，我看到了一张特别的通知。上面用大字写着：「本周六下午三点，楼下空地，社区电影放映。欢迎大家带椅子来看。」下面还画了一个笑脸。我想起小时候在农村老家，夏天的晚上大家会在院子里看露天电影。那时候的感觉是那么开心——天上有星星，旁边有邻居，银幕上是好看的故事。周六我真的搬了一把椅子下去了。来的人比我想象的多，有老人有孩子，有人还带了瓜子和水果。放映的是一部很老的电影，画面有点模糊，但是大家看得很高兴。那天晚上回家的时候，我觉得自己好像真的住在一个「社区」里了，而不只是住在一栋楼里。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān jīngguò xiǎoqū gōnggào lán de shíhou, wǒ kàn dào le yì zhāng tèbié de tōngzhī. Shàngmiàn yòng dà zì xiě zhe: 「Běn zhōuliù xiàwǔ sān diǎn, lóu xià kòngdì, shèqū diànyǐng fàngyìng. Huānyíng dàjiā dài yǐzi lái kàn.」 Xiàmiàn hái huà le yí gè xiàoliǎn. Wǒ xiǎng qǐ xiǎo shíhou zài nóngcūn lǎojiā, xiàtiān de wǎnshang dàjiā huì zài yuànzi lǐ kàn lùtiān diànyǐng. Nà shíhou de gǎnjué shì nàme kāixīn — tiān shàng yǒu xīngxing, pángbiān yǒu línjū, yínmù shàng shì hǎokàn de gùshi. Zhōuliù wǒ zhēn de bān le yì bǎ yǐzi xiàqù le. Lái de rén bǐ wǒ xiǎngxiàng de duō, yǒu lǎorén yǒu háizi, yǒu rén hái dài le guāzi hé shuǐguǒ. Fàngyìng de shì yí bù hěn lǎo de diànyǐng, huàmiàn yǒudiǎn móhu, dànshì dàjiā kàn de hěn gāoxìng. Nà tiān wǎnshang huí jiā de shíhou, wǒ juéde zìjǐ hǎoxiàng zhēn de zhù zài yí gè「shèqū」lǐ le, ér bù zhǐ shì zhù zài yí dòng lóu lǐ.
text_en: Today when I passed the compound's bulletin board, I noticed a special announcement. In big characters it read: 'This Saturday at 3 PM, open space downstairs, community movie screening. Everyone welcome — bring a chair.' Below it was a smiley face. I remembered my childhood in the countryside, when on summer evenings everyone would watch outdoor movies in the courtyard. The feeling then was so joyful — stars overhead, neighbors beside you, a good story on the screen. On Saturday I actually carried a chair downstairs. More people came than I'd imagined — elderly, children, some even bringing sunflower seeds and fruit. They screened a very old movie with slightly blurry images, but everyone enjoyed it. Walking home that evening, I felt like I truly lived in a 'community,' not just in a building.

questions.q_zh: 公告栏上的通知说什么时候放电影？
questions.q_en: When does the bulletin board say the movie will be shown?

questions.q_zh: 看完电影以后「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel after watching the movie?

options.text_en: thought the movie was too old

options.text: 觉得自己真的住在一个社区里了
options.pinyin: juéde zìjǐ zhēn de zhù zài yí gè shèqū lǐ le
options.text_en: felt like truly living in a community
wanted to move to the countryside

title: The Last Train Stranger
text_zh: 末班地铁上，坐在我对面的是一个穿着护士服的年轻女孩。她看起来非常疲倦，眼圈有点黑，但是手里拿着一个三明治在认真地吃。我猜她大概是刚下夜班。吃完三明治以后她从包里拿出一本很厚的书开始看。我注意到那是一本考试用的参考书——也许她在准备什么考试。地铁到了一个站，上来了一个抱着孩子的妈妈。孩子一直在哭，妈妈看起来也很累。那个护士把自己的座位让给了那位妈妈，然后站到了车门旁边继续看书。孩子慢慢不哭了，在妈妈怀里睡着了。我看着这个安静的画面想，这座城市的深夜，处处都有温柔的人在安静地努力着。
text_pinyin: Mòbān dìtiě shàng, zuò zài wǒ duìmiàn de shì yí gè chuān zhe hùshi fú de niánqīng nǚhái. Tā kàn qǐlái fēicháng píjuàn, yǎnquān yǒudiǎn hēi, dànshì shǒu lǐ ná zhe yí gè sānmíngzhì zài rènzhēn de chī. Wǒ cāi tā dàgài shì gāng xià yèbān. Chī wán sānmíngzhì yǐhòu tā cóng bāo lǐ ná chū yì běn hěn hòu de shū kāishǐ kàn. Wǒ zhùyì dào nà shì yì běn kǎoshì yòng de cānkǎo shū — yěxǔ tā zài zhǔnbèi shénme kǎoshì. Dìtiě dào le yí gè zhàn, shàng lái le yí gè bào zhe háizi de māma. Háizi yìzhí zài kū, māma kàn qǐlái yě hěn lèi. Nàge hùshi bǎ zìjǐ de zuòwèi ràng gěi le nà wèi māma, ránhòu zhàn dào le chē mén pángbiān jìxù kàn shū. Háizi mànmàn bù kū le, zài māma huái lǐ shuì zháo le. Wǒ kàn zhe zhège ānjìng de huàmiàn xiǎng, zhè zuò chéngshì de shēnyè, chùchù dōu yǒu wēnróu de rén zài ānjìng de nǔlì zhe.
text_en: On the last subway train, sitting across from me was a young woman in a nurse's uniform. She looked extremely tired, with slight dark circles under her eyes, but she was earnestly eating a sandwich. I guessed she'd just come off a night shift. After finishing the sandwich, she pulled a thick book from her bag and started reading. I noticed it was a test preparation reference book — perhaps she's studying for an exam. The train reached a station, and a mother holding a crying child got on. The child kept crying, and the mother looked exhausted too. The nurse gave her seat to the mother, then stood by the door and continued reading. The child gradually stopped crying and fell asleep in the mother's arms. Looking at this quiet scene, I thought: in the depths of this city's night, gentle people everywhere are quietly working hard.

questions.q_zh: 那个护士在地铁上做了什么好事？
questions.q_en: What kind thing did the nurse do on the subway?

options.text_en: helped the mother comfort the child

options.text: 把座位让给了抱孩子的妈妈
options.pinyin: bǎ zuòwèi ràng gěi le bào háizi de māma
options.text_en: gave her seat to the mother with the child

questions.q_zh: 护士在看什么样的书？
questions.q_en: What kind of book was the nurse reading?

title: The Dawn Bakery Queue
text_zh: 街角面包店早上五点半开门，但是五点就已经有人在排队了。最早来的总是那个遛狗的大爷，他每天带着他的小狗来买一个全麦面包。小狗很乖，安安静静地坐在他脚边等。第二个到的一般是对面花店的老板娘，她每天要早起开店进货，顺便买两个牛角面包当早餐。我是第三个常客，六点左右到。我们三个人每天早上都会见面，但是说的话很少，最多就是「早」和「今天天气不错」。面包师傅有时候会多做几个小饼干，免费送给排队的人。大爷总是把他那份饼干掰一半给小狗吃。清晨的面包店门口，这些简单的、重复的小事情，组成了一种让人安心的日常。
text_pinyin: Jiējiǎo miànbāo diàn zǎoshang wǔ diǎn bàn kāi mén, dànshì wǔ diǎn jiù yǐjīng yǒu rén zài páiduì le. Zuì zǎo lái de zǒng shì nàge liú gǒu de dàye, tā měi tiān dài zhe tā de xiǎo gǒu lái mǎi yí gè quánmài miànbāo. Xiǎo gǒu hěn guāi, ān'ānjìngjìng de zuò zài tā jiǎo biān děng. Dì èr gè dào de yìbān shì duìmiàn huādiàn de lǎobǎnniáng, tā měi tiān yào zǎo qǐ kāi diàn jìn huò, shùnbiàn mǎi liǎng gè niújiǎo miànbāo dāng zǎocān. Wǒ shì dì sān gè chángkè, liù diǎn zuǒyòu dào. Wǒmen sān gè rén měi tiān zǎoshang dōu huì jiànmiàn, dànshì shuō de huà hěn shǎo, zuìduō jiù shì「zǎo」hé「jīntiān tiānqì búcuò」. Miànbāo shīfu yǒu shíhou huì duō zuò jǐ gè xiǎo bǐnggān, miǎnfèi sòng gěi páiduì de rén. Dàye zǒng shì bǎ tā nà fèn bǐnggān bāi yí bàn gěi xiǎo gǒu chī. Qīngchén de miànbāo diàn ménkǒu, zhèxiē jiǎndān de, chóngfù de xiǎo shìqing, zǔchéng le yì zhǒng ràng rén ānxīn de rìcháng.
text_en: The corner bakery opens at five-thirty, but people are already queuing by five. The first to arrive is always the old man who walks his dog — he comes every day with his small dog to buy a whole wheat loaf. The dog is well-behaved, sitting quietly at his feet. The second to arrive is usually the flower shop owner from across the street, who gets up early to open shop and stock inventory, picking up two croissants for breakfast along the way. I'm the third regular, arriving around six. The three of us see each other every morning but rarely say much — at most 'morning' and 'nice weather today.' The baker sometimes makes a few extra cookies and gives them free to people in line. The old man always breaks his cookie in half for the dog. In front of the bakery at dawn, these simple, repeated little things form a kind of reassuring daily rhythm.

questions.q_zh: 最早来排队的人买什么？
questions.q_en: What does the first person in line buy?

questions.q_zh: 大爷怎么处理面包师傅送的饼干？
questions.q_en: What does the old man do with the baker's free cookies?

options.pinyin: bāi yí bàn gěi xiǎo gǒu chī

title: The Sound of Rain on the Window
text_zh: 今天晚上下了一场小雨。我关了灯，躺在床上听窗户外面的雨声。雨滴打在玻璃上的声音很轻，像有人在用手指轻轻敲门。楼下偶尔传来一两辆车经过水坑的声音，哗的一下，然后又安静了。远处好像有人在关窗户，发出咔嗒一声。我想起小时候，下雨天妈妈总是说「下雨了，今天早点睡」。那时候我不喜欢下雨，因为下雨就不能出去玩。但是现在我很喜欢雨天的晚上——世界变得安静下来，所有的声音都被雨包裹住了，变得柔和、模糊。雨声是最好的催眠曲，它不需要旋律，只需要一种均匀的、不断的、温柔的节奏。听着听着，我慢慢地睡着了。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǎnshang xià le yì chǎng xiǎo yǔ. Wǒ guān le dēng, tǎng zài chuáng shàng tīng chuānghu wàimiàn de yǔ shēng. Yǔdī dǎ zài bōlí shàng de shēngyīn hěn qīng, xiàng yǒu rén zài yòng shǒuzhǐ qīngqīng qiāo mén. Lóu xià ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì liǎng liàng chē jīngguò shuǐkēng de shēngyīn, huá de yíxià, ránhòu yòu ānjìng le. Yuǎnchù hǎoxiàng yǒu rén zài guān chuānghu, fā chū kādā yì shēng. Wǒ xiǎng qǐ xiǎo shíhou, xià yǔ tiān māma zǒng shì shuō「xià yǔ le, jīntiān zǎo diǎn shuì」. Nà shíhou wǒ bù xǐhuan xià yǔ, yīnwèi xià yǔ jiù bù néng chūqù wán. Dànshì xiànzài wǒ hěn xǐhuan yǔ tiān de wǎnshang — shìjiè biàn de ānjìng xiàlái, suǒyǒu de shēngyīn dōu bèi yǔ bāoguǒ zhù le, biàn de róuhé, móhu. Yǔ shēng shì zuì hǎo de cuīmián qǔ, tā bù xūyào xuánlǜ, zhǐ xūyào yì zhǒng jūnyún de, búduàn de, wēnróu de jiézòu. Tīng zhe tīng zhe, wǒ mànmàn de shuì zháo le.
text_en: Tonight a light rain fell. I turned off the lights and lay in bed listening to the rain outside the window. The sound of raindrops hitting the glass was very soft, like someone gently tapping on a door with their fingers. Occasionally from downstairs came the sound of a car or two splashing through a puddle — a quick swoosh, then quiet again. In the distance, someone seemed to be closing a window with a click. I remembered how as a child, on rainy days my mother would always say, 'It's raining — go to sleep early tonight.' Back then I didn't like rain because it meant I couldn't go out to play. But now I love rainy evenings — the world grows quiet, all sounds wrapped in rain, becoming soft and blurred. The sound of rain is the best lullaby. It doesn't need a melody, just an even, continuous, gentle rhythm. Listening and listening, I slowly fell asleep.

questions.q_zh: 雨滴打在玻璃上的声音像什么？
questions.q_en: What does the sound of raindrops on glass resemble?

options.text: 像有人用手指轻轻敲门
options.pinyin: xiàng yǒu rén yòng shǒuzhǐ qīngqīng qiāo mén
options.text_en: like someone gently tapping on a door

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得雨声是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think rain sounds are?
